notes: androidSourceSnapshotCommit is the Android HEAD used as the COMPLETE behavioral reference for the initial full migration. It is NOT a record of full parity.

lastAndroidCommitFullyPortedToIOS is intentionally null: the iOS app is not yet a complete functional copy of the Android app. Future sessions must continue the initial full migration (see CONTINUE-INITIAL-MIGRATION.md), not diff-only synchronization.

Differential (commit-to-commit) synchronization is NOT authoritative until FINALIZE-INITIAL-MIGRATION.md is run with explicit user confirmation and this file flips initialMigrationCompleted/incrementalUpdateModeEnabled to true and records a verified fully-ported commit.

upstreamAnkiDroidCommitOrTag records the customized fork's base (v2.25.0alpha1 / upstream 711510ff per OUR_FORK_CHANGES.md in the Android repo); the customized Android fork — not upstream AnkiDroid — is the behavioral source of truth.

ankiBackendCommit is the pinned upstream anki Rust backend (tag 25.09.2) used by the iOS app (docs/anki-backend-pin.md); the Android fork depends on anki-android-backend 0.1.64-anki25.09.2 sharing this upstream base.

Implemented so far (M1 + M2.1, CI-verified): native AI layer (Claude client, prompts, parsing, chat/creator view models, insights tip engine, AI SQLite, Keychain), SwiftUI shell, MathJax/Hebrew rendering, and the REAL Anki collection read path (deck tree + counts via the Rust backend xcframework).

Major areas still missing: reviewer queue + answer buttons, scheduler/FSRS UI, undo/bury/suspend/flags/tags, card browser, editor, statistics, filtered decks/custom study, import/export, backups, AnkiWeb sync, collection write paths, media, notifications, localization, accessibility, forced-study, and wiring AI write features + live insights to the backend.
Android repository is strictly read-only; this baseline was captured with read-only Git commands only.
